steps.title: Identify what the hypothesis predicts
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that paired leaves should show opposite-side orientation, so supporting data must demonstrate opposite-side counts exceeding same-side counts across the tested species.

steps.title: Find the choice matching the prediction
steps.content: Choice C states that all three species show more opposite-side pairs than same-side pairs (Graybark 270 > 100, Amur 150 > 70, July 200 > 100). Choices A and D mischaracterize the magnitudes, while B falsely claims all pairs are opposite-side. That matches choice C — C is correct.
